learning_outcomes.description: Outline the production principles and develop suitable production goals and targets for the animals studied
Assess the quality of production outputs from ruminants
Evaluate methods to improve the quality of production outputs from ruminants
Construct management plans including general husbandry, nutrition, reproduction and health aspects for the animals studied
Evaluate the economic viability of an enterprise
Critically evaluate a welfare issue present in Australian ruminant production system
Competently handle small and large ruminants

assessments.category: Participation/ Student contribution, Practical/ Demonstration, Quiz, Tutorial/ Problem Set
assessments.task_description:
Students must attend the practical or tutorial and complete the associated worksheet during the session (i.e. submit prior to leaving the class).

Question set 1: Doe management (week 1 - Practical) - worth 2% and assesses Learning Outcome 7

Question set 2: Measuring efficiency (week 2 - Tutorial) - worth 2% and assesses Learning Outcome 3

Question set 3: Small ruminant parasitology (week 3 - Practical) - worth 2% and assesses Learning Outcome 7

Question set 4: Stocking density (Week 4 - Tutorial) - worth 2% and assesses Learning Outcome 1

Question set 5: Acidosis in feedlots (week 6 - Tutorial) - worth 2% and assesses Learning Outcome 6

Question set 6: Goat husbandry (completed by week 6 - conducted outside of normal practical hours) - worth 3% and assesses Learning Outcome 7

Question set 7: Sheep (week 8 - Tutorial) - worth 2% and assesses Learning Outcome 6

Question set 8: Build a beast (Week 9 - Tutorial) - worth 2% and assesses Learning Outcome 1

Question set 9: Dairy (completed by week 9 - conducted outside of normal practical hours) - worth 3% and assesses Learning Outcome 7

Question set 10: ACC's Brisbane Valley Feedlot site visit (week 10 - Fieldtrip) - worth 4% and assesses Learning Outcome 1

Question set 11: ACC's Cannon Hill Processing Plant site visit (week 11 - Fieldtrip) - worth 4% and assesses Learning Outcome 2

Question set 12: Meat Quality (week 13 - Practical) - worth 2% and assesses Learning Outcome 2


This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To obtain a mark for the practical/field trip/tutorial classes students must participate and complete the associated exercises (submitting the practical report via Blackboard - Turnitin). If you do not participate in a practical class, or do not provide evidence of your attendance (i.e. signing attendance sheet, or scanning student ID), your report will not be marked/graded. Refer to late submission notes for more information. 

Absence from a Practical/Field Trip/Tutorial Session 

If you are unable to complete your timetabled practical/field trip/tutorial session because of circumstances beyond your control (e.g., illness) you must submit a practical absence request immediately accompanied by acceptable supporting documentation.  

 A list of acceptable reasons and required supporting documentation is found at https://my.uq.edu.au/information-and-services/manage-my-program/exams-and-assessment/applying-assessment-extension?p=1#1  

Your request must be submitted no later than 24 hours following the missed practical/field trip/tutorial. The submission link can be found in the Learning Resources (Blackboard Original) or Course Resources (Blackboard Ultra) folder in your course Blackboard site. 

Requests without the correct supporting documentation may not be approved. If the reason for your absence is not approved, you will be considered as absent from that session and you will not be eligible for marks from that exercise.

It is your responsibility to submit an application and provide correct documentation. If approved, alternative assessment will be organised with the individual student. Alternative assessment will be subject to late penalties, if submitted after the communicated due date.

If you require further assistance, please do not hesitate to contact the School - agriculture@enquire.uq.edu.au.
assessments.submission_guidelines: All questions will be submitted as a worksheet before leaving the class.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.assessment_title: Case study - Production Management Plan - Written report
assessments.task_description:
Students will create a management plan for a beef cattle enterprise, producing a written report targeted at the owners of the enterprise in a maximum of 2,000 words. The full details will be provided on Blackboard. In short, you will create a management plan recommending changes in the short-, medium- and long-term to assist the owners in achieving their goals. Justifications for your decisions will need to be provided using scientific literature.


Acknowledging and/or referencing AI use 

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly acknowledge any use of AI or MT in each instance.  

A failure to acknowledge generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.  

The use of AI or MT must be acknowledged appropriately. Please see assessment guidelines in blackboard course site for specific instructions. Students may also refer to Library guides found under Additional assessment information > Acknowledging and referencing AI use.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You must submit your assignment task, in Blackboard, via TurnItIn link by the submission deadline. You should also retain an electronic copy of every piece of assessment you submit.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Case study - Production Management Plan - Oral presentation
assessments.task_description:
You will give a 3 min oral presentation of your written case study, highlighting the key recommendations and economic implications of your plan (5%). This can be accompanied by slides, but it is not a requirement.

You will then participate in a 5 min Question and Answer (Q&A) session to discuss your choices and the expected outcomes (15%).

A full description of the task, and marking rubrics will be available on Blackboard.

This assessment item is not eligible for a re-mark as it is independently marked by two examiners and has undergone the prescribed moderation and review processes. As a result, there is no further academic judgement to be reconsidered through a re-mark process.

Students who believe an administrative error has occurred (for example, an error in mark calculation, data entry, or grade recording) may submit a query to the course coordinator for review. Any identified administrative errors will be corrected where appropriate.

Oral Presentation component

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly acknowledge any use of AI or MT in each instance.  

A failure to acknowledge generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.  

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission and oral presentation, independent of AI and MT tools.

The use of AI or MT must be acknowledged appropriately regarding the oral presentation. Please see assessment guidelines in blackboard course site for specific instructions. Students may also refer to Library guides found under Additional assessment information > Acknowledging and referencing AI use. 

Q&A component

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You are not required to submit anything for this assessment, all grading will occur live during the presentation and Q&A.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:

The exam will be an on-campus exam. Have your UQ student ID card available for all your exams. Students enrolled in In Person delivery must attend the exam in-person.
